title: buildah inspect has 5 values that differ from docker inspect
body:
During conformance testing it was discovered:

Five items have different values: WorkingDir, OnBuild, Hostname, Labels, Cmd when doing a buildah inspect versus a docker inspect.

Per Yiqiao Pu

[1] https://github.com/ypu/buildah/tree/conformance

Buildah 1.4

comments.body: Test with new tree. And find out the problem still exist when we set "--format docker" during the test. The pullreq effect the default value which is "--format oci" from my test.

Seems Hostname, Labels are fixed. Cmd in ContainerConfig is still different. But Cmd in config is the same. This is the details of test results. Only list items in Config here, ContainerConfig is similar:
With set --format=oci:

Field Name | Value in docker image | Value in buildah image
------------ | ------------- | -------------
Config:OnBuild | [] | nil
Config:Hostname | 394579f29896 | ""

With set --format=docker:

Field Name | Value in docker image | Value in buildah image
------------ | ------------- | -------------
Config:Entrypoint | nil | []
Config:Hostname | 08690ee29825 | 0ef136a841d7
Config:Volumes | nil | map[]
The hostname should be different, Since these are generated on the fly.

I don't see a difference here?

```
 docker inspect --format {{.Config.Volumes}} dan
map[]
./buildah inspect --format {{.Docker.Config.Volumes}} dan
map[]
```

Or here
```
# ./buildah inspect --format {{.Docker.Config.Entrypoint}} dan
[]
# docker inspect --format {{.Config.Entrypoint}} dan
[]
```

Hi @rhatdan 
Checked the image. And find if you print the value with --format {{.Config.Entrypoint}}, it will show "[]". But if you inspect the image without --format, the value will turn to "null". And also test --format {{.Config}}, it shows <nil> . So the docker inspect output seems depends on the command line flags.
We have agreement on differnces, and for the most part they match.
